# ADR 0002: Proxy Pattern for Space and Environment Scoped Operations

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Most CMA operations are scoped to a space or an environment. Without a proxy, every call must explicitly pass `$spaceId` and `$environmentId`. Alternatively, callers could fetch a full `Space` or `Environment` resource first, but that makes a redundant HTTP call just to get an ID container.

## Decision

Introduce `SpaceProxy` and `EnvironmentProxy` — lightweight objects that hold a space/environment ID and expose the same `get*/create/resolve` methods as the real resource, but without fetching the resource itself. Proxies are obtained via `$client->getSpaceProxy($spaceId)` and `$spaceProxy->getEnvironmentProxy($environmentId)`.

The proxy methods delegate to the client using the captured IDs; the real `Space`/`Environment` resources delegate back to the client through the same proxy extension traits.

Evidence: `src/Proxy/SpaceProxy.php`, `src/Proxy/EnvironmentProxy.php`, trait mixins in `src/Proxy/Extension/` and `src/ClientExtension/`.

## Consequences

- Eliminates the "fetch space just to get its ID" anti-pattern.
- Callers that already have IDs (the common case) make zero unnecessary HTTP calls.
- The proxy/resource method parity is maintained via shared traits — new resource types require updating both the proxy extension trait and the resource class.
- Slightly more complex to navigate for newcomers (methods appear on both resources and proxies via traits).
